questions.question: A pendulum makes 50 complete swings in 2 min 40 s. What is the time period for 1 complete swing? A 1.6 s B    3.2 s C    4.8 s D    6.4 s
questions.text: A pendulum makes 50 complete swings in 2 min 40 s. What is the time period for 1 complete swing? A 1.6 s B    3.2 s C    4.8 s D    6.4 s
options: A 1.6 s B    3.2 s C    4.8 s D    6.4 s

questions.question: A student investigates the motion of a ball rolling down a slope. The diagram shows the speed v of the ball at different times t. t = 0 s v = 0 cm / s t = 0.2 s v = 2.8 cm / s t = 0.4 s v = 5.1 cm / s t = 0.6 s v = 7.0 cm / s ball Which statement describes the motion of the ball? A The acceleration is not constant., B The acceleration is negative., C The speed is decreasing., D The velocity is constant.
questions.text: A student investigates the motion of a ball rolling down a slope. The diagram shows the speed v of the ball at different times t. t = 0 s v = 0 cm / s t = 0.2 s v = 2.8 cm / s t = 0.4 s v = 5.1 cm / s t = 0.6 s v = 7.0 cm / s ball Which statement describes the motion of the ball? A The acceleration is not constant., B The acceleration is negative., C The speed is decreasing., D The velocity is constant.
options: A The acceleration is not constant.

questions.question: Which statement about acceleration is correct? A It is related to the changing speed of an object., B It is the distance an object travels in one second., C It is the force acting on an object divided by the distance it travels in one second., D It is the force acting on an object when it is near to the Earth.
questions.text: Which statement about acceleration is correct? A It is related to the changing speed of an object., B It is the distance an object travels in one second., C It is the force acting on an object divided by the distance it travels in one second., D It is the force acting on an object when it is near to the Earth.
options: A It is related to the changing speed of an object.
B It is the distance an object travels in one second.
C It is the force acting on an object divided by the distance it travels in one second.
D It is the force acting on an object when it is near to the Earth.

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes the effects of placing a heavy load in a car? A It is easier to accelerate the car and easier to bring the car to rest., B It is easier to accelerate the car but more difficult to bring the car to rest., C It is more difficult to accelerate the car and more difficult to bring the car to rest., D It is more difficult to accelerate the car but easier to bring the car to rest. 3
questions.text: Which statement correctly describes the effects of placing a heavy load in a car? A It is easier to accelerate the car and easier to bring the car to rest., B It is easier to accelerate the car but more difficult to bring the car to rest., C It is more difficult to accelerate the car and more difficult to bring the car to rest., D It is more difficult to accelerate the car but easier to bring the car to rest. 3
options: A It is easier to accelerate the car and easier to bring the car to rest.
B It is easier to accelerate the car but more difficult to bring the car to rest.
C It is more difficult to accelerate the car and more difficult to bring the car to rest.
D It is more difficult to accelerate the car but easier to bring the car to rest. 3

Four solid objects are lowered in turn into the liquid. The densities of the objects are shown. 1     0.40 g / cm 3 2     0.90 g / cm 3 3     1.2 g / cm 3 4     2.7 g / cm 3 Which objects will float in the liquid? A 1    only    B    1 and 2 only C    1, 2 and 3 D    3 and 4 only

questions.question: The  diagram  shows  a  wooden  beam  PQ,  of  negligible  weight,  which  is  attached  to  a  wall  by  a hinge at P and kept in a horizontal position by a vertical rope attached at Q. Q hinge beam rope man wall P What  is  the  distance  of  the  man  from  P  when  the  tension  in  the  rope  at  Q  becomes  equal  to 500 N?, A 0.53 m B    1.1  m C    1.9  m D    2.5  m 4

questions.question: α-particles, β-particles and γ-rays are emitted by radioactive nuclei when they decay. Which emissions can be deflected by an electric field? A α-particles and β-particles only, B β-particles and γ-rays only, C γ-rays and α-particles only, D α-particles, β-particles and γ-rays
